# dgent

> De-agent your code.

dgent (de-agent) is a CLI tool that cleans AI agent output from commits and code. It runs automatically via git hooks, removing mechanical tells like Co-Authored-By trailers, emoji, AI vocabulary, section header comments, and obvious naming patterns before they land in the repo.

## Install

```
npm install -g dgent
dgent init
```

`dgent init` sets `core.hooksPath` globally. Every git commit on the machine now runs through dgent.

## How it works

Two git hooks:
- `commit-msg`: modifies the commit message file (strip trailers, emojis, normalize format)
- `pre-commit`: modifies staged files (strip section headers, emoji comments, flag naming patterns)

Both hooks call `dgent hook <type> "$@" || true` — dgent never blocks a commit. On any error, the commit proceeds unmodified.

## Rules

### Commit message rules

| Rule | Default | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| strip-trailers | on | fix | Removes Co-Authored-By, Generated-By, and AI attribution trailers. Preserves human co-author trailers. |
| strip-emojis | on | fix | Removes emoji characters from commit messages. Preserves ASCII emoticons. |
| flag-message-tone | on | flag | Warns about AI vocabulary: enhance, streamline, comprehensive, utilize, leverage, facilitate, robust, optimize. Also flags phrases: "this commit", "this change", "in order to", "aims to". Flags "Implement X to Y" as subject pattern. |
| normalize-format | on | fix | Fixes conventional commit formatting: lowercase type, remove trailing period, ensure space after colon, lowercase first word (unless acronym like URL/API). Only acts on messages using conventional commit types (feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, perf, ci, build, style, revert). |
| rewrite-message | off | fix | Calls Claude API to rewrite the message matching the repo's historical commit voice. Requires API key and ai.enabled=true. |

### Pre-commit rules (code)

| Rule | Default | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| strip-section-headers | on | fix | Removes divider comments like `// --- Init ---` or `# ===== Setup =====` in files under 500 lines. |
| strip-emoji-comments | on | fix | Strips emoji from code comments and docstrings. Does not modify emoji in string literals. |
| flag-naming | on | flag | Flags identifiers ending with Manager, Handler, Processor, Service, Factory, Builder, Validator, Controller, Orchestrator, Coordinator on declaration lines. Also flags identifiers over 40 characters. |
| flag-catch-rethrow | on | flag | Flags catch blocks that only contain console.log/error + throw with no added context. Does not flag catches that wrap errors, add metrics, or handle the error. |
| strip-noise-comments | off | fix | Removes comments where 70%+ of meaningful words appear in the next line of code. Preserves comments with information not in the code. |
| strip-obvious-docstrings | off | fix | Removes JSDoc on functions with ≤5 line bodies where the docstring restates the function name. Preserves docstrings with @param, @throws, @example tags. |
| flag-log-bracketing | off | flag | Flags pairs of console.log statements within 5 lines that narrate start/end of an operation. |

**Fix rules** modify code silently. **Flag rules** warn to stderr without modifying.

### Complete pattern lists (for agents)

**flag-message-tone words** (exact, case-insensitive): enhance, streamline, comprehensive, utilize, leverage, facilitate, robust, optimize

**flag-message-tone phrases** (substring match): "this commit", "this change", "in order to", "aims to", "is designed to"

**flag-message-tone subject patterns**: "Implement X to Y" as commit subject opener

**flag-naming suffixes**: Manager, Handler, Processor, Service, Factory, Builder, Validator, Controller, Orchestrator, Coordinator

**flag-naming identifier length**: >40 characters

**strip-trailers AI emails**: noreply@anthropic.com, github-copilot[bot], copilot@github.com, devin-ai-integration[bot], cursor[bot], codeium[bot]

**strip-trailers prefixes**: Generated-By:, Assisted-By:

**flag-log-bracketing start words**: starting, begin, doing, processing, fetching, loading, connecting

**flag-log-bracketing end words**: done, complete, completed, finished, processed, fetched, loaded, connected

**strip-obvious-docstrings preserved tags**: @param, @returns, @throws, @example, @see, @deprecated, @since, @type

For a machine-readable catalog: `dgent rules --json`

## Configuration

Global config: `~/.config/dgent/config.json`

```json
{
  "rules": {
    "strip-trailers": true,
    "strip-emojis": true,
    "strip-emoji-comments": true,
    "strip-section-headers": true,
    "normalize-format": true,
    "flag-naming": true,
    "flag-message-tone": true,
    "flag-catch-rethrow": true,
    "strip-noise-comments": false,
    "strip-obvious-docstrings": false,
    "flag-log-bracketing": false,
    "rewrite-message": false
  },
  "ai": {
    "enabled": false,
    "model": "claude-sonnet-latest",
    "skill": "bundled"
  },
  "output": {
    "verbose": false,
    "log-dir": "~/.local/share/dgent/logs"
  }
}
```

### Setting config values

```
dgent config list                              # print all values
dgent config set rules.strip-noise-comments true   # enable a rule
dgent config set rules.flag-naming false           # disable a rule
dgent config set ai.enabled true                   # enable AI layer
dgent config set api-key <key>                     # store API key securely
```

Config values use dot notation. Boolean strings are auto-converted.

### Per-repo overrides

Place `.dgent.json` in the repo root. Only the `rules` section is overridable:

```json
{
  "rules": {
    "strip-noise-comments": true,
    "flag-naming": false
  }
}
```

Repo overrides are merged on top of global config.

## Commands

```
dgent init                    # install global git hooks
dgent uninstall               # remove hooks, unset core.hooksPath
dgent scan [dir]              # scan entire directory for tells
dgent scan --fix              # scan and auto-fix
dgent run <file>              # check a single file (auto-detects phase from extension)
dgent run --fix <file>        # fix in place
dgent run --json <file>       # structured output for agents/CI
dgent run - < file            # read from stdin
dgent fix <file>              # AI-powered fix for flags (requires API key)
dgent fix --commit-msg        # AI-powered commit message fix
dgent config                  # interactive config editor (TUI)
dgent config list             # show current config
dgent config set <k> <v>     # set a config value
dgent review                  # interactive flag review (TUI)
dgent stats                   # flag trends and breakdown
dgent log                     # raw flag history
dgent log --all               # show all flag history
dgent doctor                  # check setup and common issues
dgent integrate               # install Claude Code skills
dgent rage                    # print debug info for bug reports
dgent test                    # run fixture suite (development)
dgent test --rule <name>      # run fixtures for one rule
dgent update                  # self-update via npm
```

## Dry-run mode

Preview what dgent would do without modifying anything:

```
DGENT_DRY_RUN=1 git commit -m "your message"
```

Or for manual passes:

```
dgent run --dry-run <file>
```

## AI skill layer

Optional. Requires an Anthropic API key.

```
dgent config set api-key <your-key>
dgent config set ai.enabled true
dgent config set rules.rewrite-message true
```

The API key is stored in macOS Keychain (via `security` CLI) or `~/.local/share/dgent/.key` on Linux. The `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` environment variable takes precedence if set.

The AI layer uses a bundled `skill.md` as the system prompt. Override it at `~/.config/dgent/skill.md`. The model is configurable via `ai.model`.

The AI layer is additive — if no API key is present, it's skipped silently. The deterministic rules always run regardless.

### AI autofix

With `ai.autofix` enabled, hooks automatically call Claude to resolve flags during commit:

```
dgent config set ai.autofix true
```

Renames AI identifiers, removes empty catch-rethrow, cleans AI vocabulary from messages. If the AI call fails, flags print as warnings instead (never blocks).

### Manual AI fix

```
dgent fix src/file.ts              # fix flagged code
dgent fix --commit-msg message.txt # fix flagged message
dgent fix --dry-run src/file.ts    # preview
```

## Inline ignores

Suppress specific flags with comments:

```
// dgent-ignore flag-naming
class DataProcessor { ... }           // not flagged

// dgent-ignore-next-line
class UserServiceHandler { ... }      // not flagged

const x = getValue(); // dgent-ignore // ignore all rules on this line
```

Supports `// dgent-ignore`, `// dgent-ignore-next-line`, `// dgent-ignore <rule1> <rule2>`, and `#` for Python/shell.

## Safety guarantees

1. dgent never blocks a commit. All hooks use `|| true`. On any internal error, the commit proceeds unmodified.
2. Files with mixed staged/unstaged changes are skipped entirely for diff transforms.
3. First run prompts for consent before modifying any staged files. Non-interactive environments skip automatically.
4. The rules engine targets sub-100ms execution.

## Partial staging

If a file has both staged and unstaged changes, dgent skips it for diff transforms and prints a warning. It does not attempt to merge or re-stage partial content. Commit message transforms are unaffected.

## Hook conflict detection

`dgent init` checks if `core.hooksPath` is already set. If it's set to a directory not owned by dgent (no `.dgent` marker file), init aborts and prints manual chaining instructions instead of overwriting.

## Flag logging

Flags from each commit are written to `~/.local/share/dgent/logs/` as JSON files. View with `dgent log` or `dgent review`.

## Troubleshooting

**dgent not running on commits:** Check `git config --global core.hooksPath` points to `~/.config/dgent/hooks/`.

**Rule not firing:** Check `dgent config list` to verify the rule is enabled. Check `.dgent.json` in the repo root for overrides.

**Want to skip dgent for one commit:** Use `git commit --no-verify`.

**Want to see what dgent would do:** Use `DGENT_DRY_RUN=1 git commit -m "..."`.

**Uninstall completely:** `dgent uninstall` removes hooks and unsets core.hooksPath.
